sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.content:
DE closes 2024-10-27 with $51.7B of revenue (-15.6% YoY) and $7.10B of net income (-30.2% YoY). The period ended with an operating margin of 17.5% and a net margin of 13.7%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability. Revenue and earnings both weakened, leaving the period without a clear offsetting strength on the income statement.

Operating cash flow reached $9.23B (7.5% YoY), and after $1.64B of capital spending the business produced $7.59B of free cash flow (7.1% YoY). Cash generation exceeded reported earnings, a sign that the period converted accounting profit into liquidity with relatively little friction. Capital spending absorbed 3.2% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

sections.title: Trend Analysis
sections.content:
Across the annual periods shown here, Free Cash Flow provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $6.10B and a compound annual growth rate of 8.7%. The multi-year trend is positive, but the pace is moderate rather than transformative.

Cash generation remained constructive, with $9.23B of operating cash flow translating into $7.59B of free cash flow.

sections.title: Risk Factors
sections.content: The most important risks in the current numbers are concentrated in the areas where profitability, cash generation, or balance-sheet support are not moving together. Revenue declined 15.6% versus prior year.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.content:
The most constructive part of DE's profile is straightforward: ROE of 31.8% points to very strong earnings generation relative to book equity.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2024-10-27, with the prior period set at 2023-10-29. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.rendered_content:
DE closes 2024-10-27 with $51.7B of revenue (-15.6% YoY) and $7.10B of net income (-30.2% YoY). The period ended with an operating margin of 17.5% and a net margin of 13.7%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability. Revenue and earnings both weakened, leaving the period without a clear offsetting strength on the income statement.

Operating cash flow reached $9.23B (7.5% YoY), and after $1.64B of capital spending the business produced $7.59B of free cash flow (7.1% YoY). Cash generation exceeded reported earnings, a sign that the period converted accounting profit into liquidity with relatively little friction. Capital spending absorbed 3.2% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

sections.title: Trend Analysis
sections.rendered_content:
Across the annual periods shown here, Free Cash Flow provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $6.10B and a compound annual growth rate of 8.7%. The multi-year trend is positive, but the pace is moderate rather than transformative.

Cash generation remained constructive, with $9.23B of operating cash flow translating into $7.59B of free cash flow.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.rendered_content:
The most constructive part of DE's profile is straightforward: ROE of 31.8% points to very strong earnings generation relative to book equity.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2024-10-27, with the prior period set at 2023-10-29. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.
